Configuration only

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 238px; height: 1px; padding-top: 433px; margin-left: 715px;

div.div:
Different teams own hardware
vs. software deployment

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 248, 225), rgb(30, 24, 4)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(255, 213, 79), rgb(91, 55, 0));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 948px; height: 1px; padding-top: 503px; margin-left: 5px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #37474F;
display: inline-block; font-size: 10px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#37474F, #a7b5bc); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Key Takeaway: This project uses Terraform as an orchestration engine, not as an infrastructure creator.
The null provider runs commands on remote hosts via SSH. The local provider generates configuration files. No cloud API is involved.

a.text: Text is not SVG - cannot display
